Title: [Enhancement] Flash History screen
Body:
Sometimes you miss a flash, or when you click on it, it may not load up properly; but Android has already dismissed the notification and you're no longer able to read the newsflash that caught your eye. 

What I'd like to see is a place I can go where I can view the flashes received in the last 24 to 48 hrs; in case of a device issue or something causing a notification to be cleared.

Sometimes on low memory devices Android will flat out OOM and begin killing random apps and there's nothing I can do about this. This also would be helpful to know what sources have been pushing flashes and all as well, especially as we begin to see capabilities such as multiple flash news sources and the like, and may be a useful tool for also debugging if flashes are too frequent or not and if things like check interval should be tweaked.

Title: Truncated error message; `Unable to load image. This gene`
Body:
The text displayed is only `Unable to load image. This gene` (partial "e").

Gingerbread phone with a small screen (480×800), its an old'ish phone so small everything including memory :-) The device is a http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HTC_Desire.

Message text is truncated when moving to (page 46) of a comic.

From grep'ing the source code it looks like `res\values\strings.xml` line 61:

```
<string name="dialog_page_error_text">Unable to load image. This generally happens because of an out of memory error.\n\nFor CBR/RAR files, try extracting into a folder and open the folder with a long tap.\n\nFor the rest, closing ACV and trying again after a while might help.</string>
```

Title: Crash when run app
Body:

Title: OSM API JSON parse OutOfMemoryError
Body:
Out of memory while parsing OSM API json.

Possible solutions:
- Stream json values instead of parsing all at once
- Change download window depending on available memory

Stackrace:
```
java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Failed to allocate a 17159624 byte allocation with 8388608 free bytes and 8MB until OOM, max allowed footprint 200681528, growth limit 201326592
	at java.lang.StringFactory.newStringFromBytes(StringFactory.java:178)
	at java.lang.StringFactory.newStringFromBytes(StringFactory.java:209)
	at com.github.kittinunf.fuel.core.deserializers.StringDeserializer.deserialize(StringDeserializer.kt:3)
	at com.github.kittinunf.fuel.core.DeserializableKt.awaitResponseResult(Deserializable.kt:13)
	at net.pfiers.osmfocus.service.osmapi.ApiKt$apiReq$$inlined$awaitStringResponseResult$default$1.invokeSuspend(Coroutines.kt:1)
[...]
```

Title: Crash when attempting to add task to just deleted list
Body:
How to reproduce:
1. Create new list
2. Switch to list
3. Delete list
4. Close sidebar so you stay on the just deleted list
5. Press add task floating action button
6. Mirakel crashes

There are several solutions available. To me, the sanest seems to be to not allow the sidebar to be closed after deleting the active list so that the user is forced to choose another list.

This issue is probably low priority, as there is no damage or data loss and the crash can be trivially avoided, but I still felt like reporting it.

Title: Pixelated image and crash on some devices
Body:
Hi,
I tested worldmap and am using it in a project to display a big picture.

When I test your latest code on a Galaxy S3, I can reproduce an bug:
- un-zoom quickly to the minium zoom level with a pinch gesture
- zoom quickly on a country of the map.
- you will see that the country is pixelated and is never filled with the high resolution data. And you will experience an app crash some seconds after.
  (try this manipulation a few times if no problem detected)

It's like the cache is not re-computed.

I can reproduce this bug on Galaxy S3, GalaxyS2 and Galaxy Ace.

I will investigate the issue but if you have any idea it would be very welcomed.

I notice that I've got these errors in the logcat:
E/dalvikvm-heap(19564): Out of memory on a 38047104-byte allocation.
E/InputStreamScene(19564): caught oom -- cache now at 0 percent.

I also notice these errors: 
SKIA FimgApiStretch:stretch failed
But by [browsing online](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/12068110/whats-the-reason-of-fimgapistretchstretch-failed-in-android), I can read that is only due to the Samsung phone.

Title: Korehealth Korescale G2 (QN-Scale)
Body:
**Describe the bug**_x000D_
Open scale crashes when connecting to scale_x000D_
_x000D_
**To Reproduce**_x000D_
Steps to reproduce the behavior:_x000D_
1. Pair Openscale to QN-scale_x000D_
2. Enable Bluetooth in Openscale_x000D_
3. Openscale crashes_x000D_
_x000D_
Reproduced with [latest dev version](https://github.com/oliexdev/openScale/releases/tag/dev-build): Yes_x000D_
_x000D_
**Expected behavior**_x000D_
Openscale should connect to the scale and receive data when weighing occurs_x000D_
_x000D_
**Additional Context**_x000D_
Appears to be the same issue encountered by other QN-Scales from other manufacturers_x000D_

Title: set clipboard=unnamed causes crash on large deletes
Body:
Take a fairly large file like [my vimrc](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Spidey01/terry/e5f3719344fa77a7e2543d98bf9cdffc3fe79737/.vim/vimrc) and open it in Vim Touch.

Now delete everything with :%d

Vim will crash. I assume it runs out of memory. Does not work on small files with a few lines. But does work consistently with a big file like my vimrc.

This also crashes Vim Touch if you kill everything in visual mode (e.g. ggvGx). For some reason doing :g/.*/d will not cause a crash the way normal mode commands seem to.

Observed with 2.7.1412291815 from Google Play.

Title: Not working on Samsung S10 5G HID gadget
Body:
I've used this lovely tool on another more recent Samsung device and that did work. However, on this relatively old device, it does not work due to #12 at first. It did not create the HID gadget profile as that out of memory popped out. Fortunately, following your discussion on how later android kernel source fixed that issue also works for me. I added `static int gadget_index` in the beginning and changed `device = device_create(android_class, NULL, MKDEV(0, 0), NULL, "android0");` to `device = device_create(android_class, NULL, MKDEV(0, 0), NULL, "android%d", gadget_index++);`. After that, I'm able to create a gadget as shown._x000D_

_x000D_
However, when I click on that enable button, it goes back to the disabled state automatically within 2 secs and no hidg endpoints get created. I didn't really find useful info in logs._x000D_

Title: Latency information not displayed in the list of servers
Body:
### Description:_x000D_
Occasionally, when switching between protocols or when manually refreshing the servers latency, it is observed that the latency information is missing._x000D_
I noticed the issue because sometimes the app was selecting different servers when switching protocols while "fastest server" was selected._x000D_
_x000D_
### Expected result:_x000D_
App should always display the latency information when switching between protocols or when manually refreshing the servers latency._x000D_
_x000D_
### Steps to reproduce:_x000D_
1. Install 2.8.4 or beta 2.8.6._x000D_
2. Login._x000D_
3. Make sure the latency values are present in the list of servers._x000D_
4. Select fastest server._x000D_
5. Refresh the servers latency repeatedly until the issue occurs._x000D_
6. Alternatively, change the protocols several times, from WireGuard to OpenVPN and vice versa, until the issue occurs._x000D_
_x000D_
### Environment:_x000D_
IVPN: 2.8.4 or beta 2.8.6_x000D_
OS: Samsung Galaxy S10 Android 11

Title: PSA: Dealing with Invalid Token
Body:
I've had the EteSync client fail to sync contacts and calendars for a few weeks with the "Authentication failed" message. When trying to access the "dashboard" (account -> settings -> account dashboard), the app would simply popup with "Invalid token"._x000D_
_x000D_
This error has been encountered by others in the past years, causing data loss:_x000D_
https://github.com/etesync/android/issues/189_x000D_
https://github.com/etesync/android/issues/186_x000D_
https://github.com/etesync/android/issues/119_x000D_
_x000D_
[Here](https://github.com/etesync/android/issues/186#issuecomment-1159805382) says to logout, but there's no "logout" function. Searching for code with logout functionality points to https://github.com/etesync/android/commit/5857ed51d0c743f7072d22e03e2073a91ecf7bc9 which is part of the `deleteAccount` method._x000D_
_x000D_
Deleting an account would delete all unsynchronized data on the device._x000D_
_x000D_
Here's a workaround: changing the encryption password will force the app to re-acquire a token._x000D_
I didn't check why the old password (token?) was accepted while doing the change, but hey.._x000D_
_x000D_
Regards

Title: The message isn't persisted when the app is paused
Body:
In the double screen rotation test to detect data loss, an application failure was identified: message loss._x000D_ _x000D_
_x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_
_x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ The occurrence of data loss is a common problem that arises when important data is unintentionally deleted or when state variables are mistakenly assigned with default or initial values. This issue is closely related to the lifecycle of the activity (1). During a screen rotation, for example, the activity is destroyed and resumed, and if the developer fails to save the variables before destruction and restore them after creation, data loss is likely to occur. To prevent such data loss, developers must implement the logic necessary to save the activity state in the onSaveInstanceState() callback method and restore its state in the onRestoreInstanceState() callback method._x000D_ _x000D_ Another effective approach to avoiding data loss problems is to utilize the view model, which is specifically designed to store and manage UI-related data in a lifecycle-conscious manner (2). By employing this technique, developers can minimize the risk of data loss and ensure the smooth functioning of their applications._x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ References:_x000D_ _x000D_ https://developer.android.com/guide/components/activities/activity-lifecycle_x000D_ https://developer.android.com/topic/libraries/architecture/viewmodel_x000D_ _x000D_
Description: During screen rotation, activities are destroyed and recreated, and data loss is likely to occur if developers fail to save variables before destruction and restore them after creation

_x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ - Device: Pixel 2 with OS: Android 11_x000D_ _x000D_ The occurrence of data loss is a common problem that arises when important data is unintentionally deleted or when state variables are mistakenly assigned with default or initial values. This issue is closely related to the lifecycle of the activity (1). During a screen rotation, for example, the activity is destroyed and resumed, and if the developer fails to save the variables before destruction and restore them after creation, data loss is likely to occur. To prevent such data loss, developers must implement the logic necessary to save the activity state in the onSaveInstanceState() callback method and restore its state in the onRestoreInstanceState() callback method._x000D_ _x000D_ Another effective approach to avoiding data loss problems is to utilize the view model, which is specifically designed to store and manage UI-related data in a lifecycle-conscious manner (2). By employing this technique, developers can minimize the risk of data loss and ensure the smooth functioning of their applications._x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ References:_x000D_ _x000D_ 1. https://developer.android.com/guide/components/activities/activity-lifecycle_x000D_ 2. https://developer.android.com/topic/libraries/architecture/viewmodel_x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_
Description: During screen rotation, activities are destroyed and recreated, and data loss is likely to occur if developers fail to save variables before destruction and restore them after creation

Title: Fix data loss in connection with geo: intents.
Body:
When geo: intents are handled by activities created
in a different task, that can lead to the same map
being opened multiple times (once in each task).
The way the maps are implemented, this means that the
last to call onPause/onDestroy will "win" its reference
points will end up on disk.
This is particularly annoying if that one is the one
that has no reference points and you lost all
that you added...
Solve it via a horrible hack that always uses a new, clean
task (of which there should be only one).

Signed-off-by: Reimar Döffinger Reimar.Doeffinger@gmx.de

Title: A data loss issue needs confirmation
Body:
Hi~ _x000D_
We are engaged in the work of data loss detection, and we have manually confirmed some data loss issues._x000D_
However, there exist a data loss issue that we cannot confirm, and the issue detail is:_x000D_
    The affected UI is  _com.knirirr.beecount.CountOptionsActivity_, and the affected widget is the variable _count_mult_widget_ of `OptionsWidget`  type. _x000D_
_x000D_
when we try to reproduce the data loss issue by rotate the phone, what confuses us is that we cannot find this widget on UI. The appearance of it may depend on `pref_multiplier` in `onResume()`. Because we may not familiar with the app, so , could you give some advice about how to let _count_mult_widget_ appear on the UI? Thanks a lot._x000D_
_x000D_
source code excerpt from  `onResume()`._x000D_
```_x000D_
if (pref_multiplier)_x000D_
    {_x000D_
      count_mult_widget = new OptionsWidget(this, null);_x000D_
      count_mult_widget.setInstructions(getString(R.string.countMult));_x000D_
      count_mult_widget.setParameterValue(count.multiplier);_x000D_

Title: fix for losing data during rotation of device
Body:
Hello again Knirirr_x000D_
_x000D_
I was using the BeeCount app and I noticed another activity that can lead to data loss. When the user goes to edit the count settings for an item as can be seen here: https://ibb.co/N2LHbNJ_x000D_
_x000D_
If the screen focus goes to another app or activity(for example if an incoming call forces the user to go into the phone app), or if the user rotates the phone and changes the app to landscape mode, the user will lose any data they had put into this page as can be seen here: https://ibb.co/C09QQCf_x000D_
_x000D_
I have used savedInstanceState to automatically save the data in such cases and restore the data when the user returns to the BeeCount app(and hence the activity) or after the phone is rotated. Therefore, the user does not have to fill in the data again thus improving the user experience._x000D_
_x000D_
I have tested out the feature to ensure it works._x000D_
_x000D_
If you have any questions or if you would like me to change anything, please do not hesitate to let me know!_x000D_
_x000D_
Thank you for your time,_x000D_
Tim
Description: If the screen focus shifts to another application or activity (e.g., an incoming call forces the user into the Phone app), or if the user rotates the phone and switches the app to landscape mode, the user will lose any data they entered on this page. Use savedInstanceState to automatically save the data in this case and restore it after the user returns to the BeeCount app (and activity) or the phone is rotated

Title: Data loss on double screen rotation
Body:
In the double screen rotation test to detect data loss, an application failure was identified: the pop-up menu disappeared after double rotation._x000D_ _x000D_ A data loss problem occurs when data is accidentally deleted or state variables are accidentally assigned with default or initial values. This issue is related to the activity's lifecycle (1). When a screen rotation occurs, the activity is destroyed and resumed. Data loss will happen if the developer does not save the variables before the destruction and restore them after creation. To avoid this, the developers have to implement both the logic necessary to save the activity state in the onSaveInstanceState() callback method and the logic to resume its state in the onRestoreInstanceState() callback method._x000D_ Another way to avoid data loss problems is to use the view model, designed to store and manage UI-related data in a lifecycle-conscious way (2)._x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_
_x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_
_x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ Note:_x000D_ _x000D_ - Double screen rotation: It is executed twice to reach a state that should be exactly the same as the screen before if no data loss occurred._x000D_ - A data loss problem occurs when data is accidentally deleted or state variables are accidentally assigned with default or initial values._x000D_ _x000D_ 1. https://developer.android.com/guide/components/activities/activity-lifecycle_x000D_ 2. https://developer.android.com/topic/libraries/architecture/viewmodel_x000D_
Description: During screen rotation, activities are destroyed and recreated, and data loss is likely to occur if developers fail to save variables before destruction and restore them after creation

Title: Can't save after reloading some files
Body:
If we have more than one opened tab, when we close 920 text-editor, and re-open it after, the tabs reopen correctly if we checked the option to.

But when we edit the files, the title tab won't highlight in red and if we hit the save button it won't save anything.

If we switch to another tab and then switch back to the original tab, then the tabs of the edited files will turn in red, but it won't be possible again to save (if we exit the editor it won't save anything either, nor warn about possible data loss).

The current workaround is to either reload the files, or simply hit the "load" button, then exit the select file dialog: the expected behavior will come back and it will be possible to save the files again.

I experienced this bug on a nexus 4 running cyanogen mod (android 4.2.2), and a nexus 7 running vanilla google android (4.2.2 as well)

(using version 13.7.18, latest on the play store)

Title: Data loss on double screen rotation
Body:
_x000D_ In the double screen rotation test to detect data loss, an application failure was identified: the pop-up menu disappeared after double rotation._x000D_ _x000D_ A data loss problem occurs when data is accidentally deleted or state variables are accidentally assigned with default or initial values. This issue is related to the activity's lifecycle (1). When a screen rotation occurs, the activity is destroyed and resumed. Data loss will happen if the developer does not save the variables before the destruction and restore them after creation. To avoid this, the developers have to implement both the logic necessary to save the activity state in the onSaveInstanceState() callback method and the logic to resume its state in the onRestoreInstanceState() callback method._x000D_ Another way to avoid data loss problems is to use the view model, designed to store and manage UI-related data in a lifecycle-conscious way (2)._x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_

Title: Synchronize existing calendars and contacts
Body:
How does a real person use this program?_x000D_
I already have an address book with 300 entries. And a calendar with 3,000 entries. How do I synchronize the data I already have?_x000D_
_x000D_
I used to use DAVx⁵. But it works very poorly._x000D_
Their concept of storing contacts in a separate special address book is _very_ bad._x000D_
The reasons:_x000D_
1) migrating data from one address book to another address book is complicated, hard, and there is a risk of data loss. (migrating data from one container to another was a separate quest available only to programmers)_x000D_
2) as my practice has shown it is unreliable. Because of many bugs in their program, I was forced to install the previous version. And at the time of its installation all the data of the program was erased. Because of this I lost all my contacts. (It's good that I didn't trust their program and made other backups)._x000D_
3) One day there was a failure in their program and it lost _all_ its data. It happened on a business trip, in another country, I had no internet and no computer. It was a real nightmare!_x000D_
4) After losing the data on the Android side, there was a synchronization of data deletion to the server! - this is real madness, the backup program loses data and then erases the backup on the server..._x000D_
_x000D_
As a result, a program that was supposed to increase the reliability and ease of working with my data gives the opposite effect - working with data becomes more complicated and data is lost many times._x000D_
_x000D_
Please don't make the same mistake they did._x000D_
_x000D_
Translated with www.DeepL.com/Translator (free version)_x000D_
_x000D_
_x000D_
_x000D_
--------_x000D_
_x000D_
**RUS:**_x000D_
Я уже имею адресную книгу с 300 записями. И календарь с 3000 записей. Как мне синхронизировать эти уже имеющиеся данные?_x000D_
_x000D_
Раньше я пользовался DAVx⁵. Но он очень плохо работает._x000D_
Их концепция хранения контактов в отдельной специальной адресной книге - это _очень_ плохо._x000D_
Причины:_x000D_
1) произвести миграцию данных из одной адресной книги в другую адресную книгу - это сложно, тяжело и есть риск потери данных. (миграция данных из одного контейнера в другой - это был отдельный квест доступный только для программистов)_x000D_
2) как показала моя практика это ненадежно. Изза множества ошибок в их программе я был вынужден установить предыдущую версию. И в момент ее установки были стерты все данные программы. Из-за этого я потерял все контакты. (хорошо что я не доверял их программе и делал другие бэкапы)._x000D_
3) Однажды в их программе произошел сбой, и она потеряла _все_ свои данные. Это произошло в командировке, в другой стране, у меня не было интернета и компьютера. Это был настоящий кошмар!_x000D_
4) После потери данных на стороне Android происходила синхронизация удаления данных на сервер! - это настоящее сумасшествие, программа для бэкапа теряет данные и затем стирает бэкап на сервере..._x000D_
_x000D_
В итоге программа которая должна была повышать надежность и простоту работы с моими данными дает обратный эффект - работа с данными усложняется, а данные множество раз терялись._x000D_
_x000D_
Пожалуйста не повторяйте их ошибку._x000D_

Data loss will happen if the developer does not save the variables before the destruction and restore them after creation. To avoid this, the developers have to implement both the logic necessary to save the activity state in the onSaveInstanceState() callback method and the logic to resume its state in the onRestoreInstanceState() callback method._x000D_ Another way to avoid data loss problems is to use the view model, designed to store and manage UI-related data in a lifecycle-conscious way (2)._x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ Note:_x000D_ _x000D_ - Double screen rotation: It is executed twice to reach a state that should be exactly the same as the screen before if no data loss occurred._x000D_ - A data loss problem occurs when data is accidentally deleted or state variables are accidentally assigned with default or initial values._x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ _x000D_ 1. https://developer.android.com/guide/components/activities/activity-lifecycle _x000D_ 2. https://developer.android.com/topic/libraries/architecture/viewmodel_x000D_ _x000D_
Description: During screen rotation, activities are destroyed and recreated, and data loss is likely to occur if developers fail to save variables before destruction and restore them after creation

Title: Make polling intervall configurable
Body:
Like for the sensitivites, provide something like:
- very often (most secure but highest battery drain)
- often
- ...

- custom
Description: Older versions of the OS only had four polling frequencies: SENSOR_DELAY_NORMAL (5 times per second), SENSOR_DELAY_UI (16.7 times per second), SENSOR_DELAY_GAME (50 times per second) and SENSOR_DELAY_FASTEST (as fast as possible). Title: The least-ping strategy causes an infinite loop and app crashes
Body:
**Before submitting an issue, please make sure you are using the latest version of SagerNet.**_x000D_
Tested on 0.8.1-rc03_x000D_
_x000D_
### Describe the bug_x000D_
When we have a balancer with the **Least Ping** strategy, the Loopback mechanism causes a severe problem in the device. I tested it on several devices. and the result was the same. Overheating, battery drain, memory overflow, and application crashes are some side effects._x000D_
_x000D_
### To Reproduce_x000D_
In an actual situation, you can see the problem If your firewall blocks all profiles in the balancer. For testing, you can reproduce the bug with an invalid profile in the balancer._x000D_
_x000D_
Steps to reproduce the behavior:_x000D_
_x000D_
1- Create a blocked/invalid profile as VMess type_x000D_
2- Create a balancer profile with a ping strategy_x000D_
3- Add VMess profile to the balancer list_x000D_
4- Select the balancer and start the app_x000D_
5- Booom. Watch out _x000D_
_x000D_
### Debug Information_x000D_
_x000D_
> About -> Export Debug Information_x000D_
The log file is attached._x000D_
[SagerNet 5825468551351236100.zip](https://github.com/SagerNet/SagerNet/files/11245259/SagerNet.5825468551351236100.zip)_x000D_
_x000D_
_x000D_
### Expected behavior_x000D_
_x000D_
This bug makes the **Least Ping** strategy useless. The random strategy is not efficient enough. **This is a serious problem**_x000D_
I expected the proxy connection to fail when all the profiles were failed. _x000D_
_x000D_
### Screenshots_x000D_
_x000D_
![photo_2023-04-17_02-59-49](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4160541/232354132-eca3dfb4-95f0-42d9-9dae-29230c3d152d.jpg)_x000D_
_x000D_
### Smartphone_x000D_
_x000D_
- Kernel version: 4.14.117_x000D_
- Device: Xiaomi Mi 9T Pro_x000D_
- OS: Android 10 _x000D_
_x000D_
### Additional context_x000D_
_x000D_
I guess this is a v2ray-core bug. because it seems the Loopback protocol has no exit way something like retries limitation in the DNS routine. Although it can be a bug of the Least Ping strategy too._x000D_
_x000D_
I had a similar problem in HTTP-Injector with v2ray-core._x000D_
_x000D_
[Added loopback rule](https://github.com/SagerNet/SagerNet/commit/6d3160a3cff8273ea56786b684e1b902cec80c9d#diff-44ac1260a010a4fa941d1e4ed522326b68bcabe9caab3941304f28943d2bf666R875-R881) in the routes makes an **INFINITE LOOP** in the program and disaster will dawn._x000D_
_x000D_
_x000D_
I don't know why a loopback is needed in the route rules. if it is necessary for random strategy but it can cause this bug in the least ping strategy._x000D_
_x000D_
## As a developer: YOUR JOB IS WONDERFUL. you are awesome guys. your project is fantastic. Please continue._x000D_
I'm not a mobile developer. I'm sorry I can't help in this amazing project.

Title: misuse of AsyncTask
Body:
According to our research, there are misuses about the following AsyncTask class:_x000D_
`de.geeksfactory.opacclient.frontend.LibraryListActivity.LoadLibrariesTask`_x000D_
_x000D_
The problems are:_x000D_
1. `LoadLibrariesTask` is an inner class of `LibraryListActivity`, which means it holds strong reference the Activity, which can lead to memory leak when the Activity is destroyed and the AsyncTask did not finish;_x000D_
2. The instance of `LoadLibrariesTask` are not cancelled before the Activity is destroyed, which can lead to the wrong invocation of `onPostExecute()` if the Activity is destroyed_x000D_
3. The `doInBackground()` did not check the status of AsyncTask. _x000D_
_x000D_
I think we can make following changes to fix the misuse problems:_x000D_
1. `LoadLibrariesTask` should be a static inner class._x000D_
2. `loadLibrariesTask`, the instance of `LibraryListActivity` should invoke `cancel()` in the `onDestroy()` method of `LibraryListActivity` if it is not null_x000D_
3. In the loop of `doInBackground()` method, check whether AsyncTask is cancelled via `isCancelled()`. If current AsyncTask is cancelled, jump out of the loop._x000D_
_x000D_
These are my suggestions above, thank you.
Description: LoadLibrariesTask is an inner class of LibraryListActivity, which means that it holds a strong reference to the Activity, which can lead to memory leaks when the Activity is destroyed and the AsyncTask has not completed; there is no cancellation of the LoadLibrariesTask instance before the Activity is destroyed, which could result in an incorrect call to onPostExecute() when the Activity is destroyed.

Title: Activity memory leak caused by anonymous threads
Body:
An anonymous inner class will hold a reference to the this pointer of the outer class and will not be released until the thread ends._x000D_
_x000D_
It will hold the Activity and prevent its timely release._x000D_
_x000D_
```_x000D_
com.gimranov.zandy.app.MainActivity:_x000D_
 void onClick()_x000D_
 void onNewIntent()_x000D_
 void toastError()  // ApplicationContext instead_x000D_
```_x000D_
The anonymous thread can be replaced by static class with weak references of activity.
Description: An anonymous inner class holds a reference to the this pointer of the outer class and does not release it until the end of the thread.

Title: Activity memory leak caused by anonymous threads
Body:
# Possible Memory Leak_x000D_
An [anonymous inner class](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/17778077/would-anonymous-handler-or-runnable-create-a-memory-leak) will hold a reference to the this pointer of the outer class and will not be released until the thread ends._x000D_
It will hold the Activity and prevent its timely release. Please check the links below._x000D_
_x000D_
# Occurrences_x000D_
https://github.com/SubhamTyagi/android-ocr/blob/master/app/src/main/java/io/github/subhamtyagi/ocr/MainActivity.java#L314_x000D_
_x000D_
# Possible Solution_x000D_
If it is necessary, it can be changed to [static class + weak reference](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/22162642/will-handler-inside-a-method-leak-memory/27825703#27825703) to eliminate the reference to the activity, which may cause memory leaks._x000D_
Further discussion is welcome.
Description: An anonymous inner class holds a reference to the this pointer of the outer class and does not release it until the end of the thread.

Title: Misuse about AsyncTask
Body:
<!--_x000D_
Thanks for reporting issues back to us! _x000D_
_x000D_
To make it possible for us to help you please fill out below information carefully._x000D_
--> _x000D_
_x000D_
According to our research, there are misuses about the following two AsyncTask classes:_x000D_
1. fr.unix_experience.owncloud_sms.engine.ASyncSMSRecovery.SMSRecoveryTask_x000D_
2. fr.unix_experience.owncloud_sms.engine.ASyncContactLoad.ContactLoadTask_x000D_
_x000D_
The problems are:_x000D_
1. They hold strong reference to the GUI element of Activity, which can lead to memory leak when the Activity is destroyed and the AsyncTask did not finish_x000D_
2. Their instances are not cancelled before the Activity is destroyed, which can lead to the wrong invocation of onPostExecute_x000D_
_x000D_
I think we can make following changes to fix the misuse problems:_x000D_
1. I think the GUI-related fields should be wrapped into WeakReference. Take `private final RestoreMessagesActivity _context` as example, it can be changed tp `private final WeakReference<RestoreMessagesActivity> _context`._x000D_
2. Add a AsyncTask field in the corresponding Activities which use AsyncTask. These field refer to the annoymous AsyncTask object such as `new ASyncSMSRecovery.SMSRecoveryTask(me, _account).execute()`. Then invoke `cancel()` in the onDestroy() method of Activities._x000D_
_x000D_
These are my suggestions above, thank you._x000D_
Description:
They hold strong references to the Activity's GUI elements, which can lead to memory leaks when the Activity is destroyed and the AsyncTask has not completed.
Their instances are not cancelled before the Activity is destroyed, which may result in an incorrect call to onPostExecute.

Title: Activity memory leak caused by anonymous threads
Body:
An anonymous inner class will hold a reference to the this pointer of the outer class and will not be released until the thread ends._x000D_
_x000D_
We have detected that your implementation has a lot of reading and writing data in anonymous threads of the Activity, and these threads will hold the Activity and prevent its timely release._x000D_
_x000D_
ch.hgdev.toposuite.points.PointsImporterDialog_x000D_
ch.hgdev.toposuite.jobs.JobsActivity_x000D_
ch.hgdev.toposuite.points.PointsImporterActivity_x000D_
ch.hgdev.toposuite.jobs.JobImporterActivity_x000D_
_x000D_
Some of the anonymous threads can be replaced by static class to eliminate unnecessary references._x000D_
And some can be replaced by the Handler class._x000D_
_x000D_
If you agree, a PR will be submitted.
Description: An anonymous inner class holds a reference to the this pointer of the outer class and does not release it until the end of the thread.

Title: Activity memory leak caused by the anonymous thread
Body:
An anonymous inner class will hold a reference to the this pointer of the outer class and will not be released until the thread ends._x000D_
It will hold the Activity and prevent its timely release._x000D_
_x000D_
In [DrugListActivity2.java](https://github.com/jclehner/rxdroid/blob/stable/src/main/java/at/jclehner/rxdroid/DrugListActivity2.java), there is a timed task._x000D_
It can be improved by TimerTask elegantly and efficiently._x000D_
```_x000D_
timer = _x000D_
timerTask = timerTask(){_x000D_
  run(){_x000D_
  }_x000D_
}_x000D_
timer.schedule(timerTask,10,1100);_x000D_
_x000D_
onDestroy(){_x000D_
 timer.cancel();_x000D_
}_x000D_
_x000D_
```

Title: Activity memory leak caused by anonymous threads
Body:
# Possible Memory Leak_x000D_
An [anonymous inner class](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/17778077/would-anonymous-handler-or-runnable-create-a-memory-leak) will hold a reference to the this pointer of the outer class and will not be released until the thread ends._x000D_
It will hold the Activity and prevent its timely release. Please check the links below._x000D_
_x000D_

Title: Activity memory leak caused by anonymous threads
Body:
# Possible Memory Leak_x000D_
An [anonymous inner class](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/17778077/would-anonymous-handler-or-runnable-create-a-memory-leak) will hold a reference to the this pointer of the outer class and will not be released until the thread ends._x000D_
It will hold the Activity and prevent its timely release. Please check the links below._x000D_
_x000D_
# Occurrences_x000D_
https://github.com/seoulcodingcafe/RandomFileMaker/blob/master/app/src/main/java/io/github/randomfilemaker/LicenseActivity.java#L57_x000D_
_x000D_
# Possible Solution_x000D_
If it is necessary, it can be changed to [static class + weak reference](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/22162642/will-handler-inside-a-method-leak-memory/27825703#27825703) to eliminate the reference to the activity, which may cause memory leaks._x000D_
Further discussion is welcome.
Description: An anonymous inner class holds a reference to the this pointer of the outer class and does not release it until the end of the thread.

Title: Pagination
Body:
Currently the app always loads all files form the specified folder(s). In cases where this results in a lot of files (~10k) this process requires a feelable amount of time. Another issue with this approach is, although yet not reported by anybody, the memory consumption of the app. Going form a few files to ~10k loaded files results in almost doubling our memory footprint. In numbers from around 150M to 300M. So a good guess would be that with around 30k files we would run dangerously close to Android killing the app for overusing the memory._x000D_
_x000D_
A solution for this is to introduce pagination instead of loading all files at once. This, however, comes with a lot of open issues. We have to touch almost every part of the app for this to work. Any kind of filtering and searching has now to be done by the Nextcloud server to ensure reliable and reproducible results._x000D_
_x000D_
This issue will be used to collect all the relevant issues and general ideas for solving this long term._x000D_
_x000D_
The following things will have to be done:_x000D_
- Read up on WebDav and Nextcloud support for paging._x000D_
- Validate/Contribute paging support to Nextcloud Dart lib._x000D_
- Delete Syncs have to be solved based on the Activity log instead of file deltas. #77 _x000D_
- Quality checks have to be improved before starting this #9/#8 _x000D_
- Search has to request results from server instead of performing local filtering._x000D_
- Offline mode will still load everything? What is the fallback strategy? How does search behave in offline mode?
Description: It can be surmised that when the number of files increases to about 30,000, we may be close to the danger of having the application terminated by Android due to memory overuse. The solution to this problem is to introduce a paging mechanism instead of loading all the files at once

Title: Resource leak -- file not closed -- found by Facebook's static analyzer
Body:
Hello,
This resource leak report, included with comments
below, were found by running 

Facebook's Infer static analyzer on RemoteDroid.

Regards,
Dulma Rodriguez
Facebook Static Analysis Tools Team

File: app/src/main/java/com/koushikdutta/async/util/StreamUtility.java

Report: Resource Leak: resource acquired by call to FileOutputStream(...) at line 83 is not released after line 86.

Remarks: The problem seems to be that dout.write(...) can throw an exception, in which case dout.close() won't get called. A simple fix is to put the call to close in a finally clause.
Description: By calling FileOutputStream(...) at line 83 Resource fetched after line 86 is not released

Title: memory leak about AsyncTask
Body:
According to our research, there are misuses about the following AsyncTask class:_x000D_
( in com.tananaev.logcat. MainActivity)_x000D_
```_x000D_
private ReaderTask readerTask;_x000D_
//……_x000D_
private class ReaderTask extends AsyncTask<Void, StatusUpdate, Void> _x000D_
```_x000D_
The problems is:_x000D_
1. It is inner class. It holds strong reference to the GUI element of Activity( MainActivity ), which can lead to memory leak when the Activity is destroyed and the AsyncTask did not finish._x000D_
_x000D_
I think we can make following changes to fix the misuse problems:_x000D_
1. I think the GUI-related fields should be wrapped into WeakReference. Take _x000D_
`private final MainActivity _context` as example, it can be changed to `private final WeakReference<MainActivity> _context`.
Description: This is an internal class. It holds a strong reference to the GUI element of the activity (MainActivity), which can lead to memory leaks when the activity is destroyed and the AsyncTask has not completed.

```_x000D_
_x000D_
Besides,  the while loop in the child thread should check the interrupt status of the thread, and the activity should interrupt the thread to end unnecessary loop execution before destroying it._x000D_
The anonymous thread can be replaced by static class to eliminate unnecessary references._x000D_

Title: "Follow" an app when it crashes or is killed by Android
Body:
Hi, I'm using AnotherMonitor to help track memory leaks in another app I use. Since this app uses a lot of memory, it is often shut down by the system, and sometimes I also forget to re-add the app after it crashes._x000D_
_x000D_
When it next crashes again, this means I have no memory trace of the app for debugging. Please consider adding a "Follow" feature, so that when an app crashes, and is later restarted as a new PID, AnotherMonitor automatically starts watching this new PID using the same graph line.

Title: Activity memory leak caused by anonymous threads
Body:
# Possible Memory Leak_x000D_
An [anonymous inner class](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/17778077/would-anonymous-handler-or-runnable-create-a-memory-leak) will hold a reference to the this pointer of the outer class and will not be released until the thread ends._x000D_
It will hold the Activity and prevent its timely release. Please check the links below._x000D_
_x000D_
# Occurrences_x000D_
https://github.com/snikket-im/snikket-android/blob/master/src/main/java/eu/siacs/conversations/ui/RecordingActivity.java#L140_x000D_
https://github.com/snikket-im/snikket-android/blob/master/src/main/java/eu/siacs/conversations/ui/RtpSessionActivity.java#L320_x000D_
_x000D_
# Possible Solution_x000D_
If it is necessary, it can be changed to [static class + weak reference](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/22162642/will-handler-inside-a-method-leak-memory/27825703#27825703) to eliminate the reference to the activity, which may cause memory leaks._x000D_
Further discussion is welcome.
Description: An anonymous inner class holds a reference to the this pointer of the outer class and does not release it until the end of the thread.

Title: [FEATURE] - Local HTTP/SOCKS5 proxy mode for use with other VPNs
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**_x000D_
For quite a long time now I've always been torn between using the best ad blocker for Android, AdGuard, and using Cloudflare Warp via WireGuard for faster internet (no video buffering, less ping in video games, etc). I can't use both because Android only allows one VPN active at a time. AdGuard does have an automatic proxy mode for rooted devices to allow using WireGuard with it though, but ad filtering will not work if an ipv6 address is specified in the WireGuard config, which must result in me essentially connecting to ipv4 only, in order to continue experiencing the best of both AdGuard and Cloudflare Warp._x000D_
_x000D_
**Describe the solution you'd like**_x000D_
A mode in WG Tunnel that allows exposing configs to other compatible VPNs, like AdGuard, in a form of a local (127.0.0.1:xxxx) HTTP and/or SOCKS5 inbound proxy. In this manner, AdGuard, in it's regular VPN mode can filter ads in both ipv4 and ipv6 connections like normal, but through the proxy, so that I may also experience the benefits of Cloudflare Warp._x000D_
_x000D_
**Describe alternatives you've considered**_x000D_
- Kernel userspace mode in WireGuard: with that enabled, whether AdGuard is in VPN mode or automatic proxy, the same undesirable results occur so long as an ipv6 config is specified in the WireGuard config._x000D_
- SagerNet (depreciated; based on v2-ray) & successor, sing-box (based on sing-box): both SagerNet and sing-box cannot refresh its proxy connection automatically, so when the Android device regains cell service or connects/disconnects from Wifi, all apps not excluded from AdGuard filtering in VPN mode will not connect to the internet until the responsible proxy app is manually stopped and restarted._x000D_
- SagerNet fork, Matsuri (depreciated; based on v2-ray) & successor, NekoBoxForAndroid (based on sing-box): Matsuri, like SagerNet, did not well support WireGuard configs, as the app's base arch, v2-ray, does not support WireGuard configs at all, so connections were often unstable; NekoBoxForAndroid, like both Matsuri and SagerNet, 1): have a lot more settings that I deem unnecessary, unappealing, and just confusing for my use case, that, if tampered with, with limited proper knowledge of them, can drastically affect the connection for better or worse, and 2): are simply battery drainers compared to sing-box and WireGuard itself._x000D_
_x000D_
**Additional context**_x000D_
I recently found another GitHub project called [Wireproxy](https://github.com/pufferffish/wireproxy). I haven't tried it, but, given the description of the project, it seems to present the solution related to my issue, it's just not made for Android. Hopefully it's content helps though._x000D_

Title: Improve full payload mode
Body:
The current implementation of the full payload option is quite limiting, as it's almost "all or nothing". With the introduction of the decryption rules, since the TLS decryption can now happen during normal monitoring, it's often necessary to review some decrypted data in full while avoiding the app from going out of memory because of the full payload._x000D_
_x000D_
In essence we need a more dynamic approach, here are some ideas:_x000D_
- give the user the ability to selectively enable the full payload at runtime. Also possibly evaluate to enable it always for decrypted connections_x000D_
- always keep the most recent x MB of payload in memory, so that the user can always review the most recent connections payload_x000D_
- possibly implement an alternative file-backed payload dump to overcome the ram limits

Title: replace ->,<- and <-> by the matching arrow characters in super notes
Body:
# What_x000D_
This PR adds an automatic replacement in the super editor when typing `-> `, `<- ` or `<-> ` by the corresponding arrow character._x000D_
_x000D_
# Why_x000D_
Personally I use a lot of → arrows in my texts to indicate a flow or an outcome of an action. The arrow characters are visually more pleasing to use, but are more difficult to type. This feature is present in the [Rich Markdown Editor](https://github.com/arturolinares/sn-rme), which I have used almost exclusively for the last couple years in StandardNotes. This automatic replacement speeds up the typing / search for the characters to copy and paste._x000D_
_x000D_
# Thoughts_x000D_
The replacement is triggered when typing a space character, so shouldn't be a problem in case the characters are used inside of any string. The replacement only happens at the current position of typing, not in multiple places in the same line that all get matched with the regex. Without the $ in the regex the cursor would jump to the first match in the line and not replace the currently typed characters._x000D_
_x000D_
I used the [emoji plugin](https://github.com/facebook/lexical/blob/main/packages/lexical-playground/src/plugins/EmojisPlugin/index.ts) from the lexical playground as a guide of how to implement this feature, because it does something very similar. However, I chose not to implement a list/map for the different arrows like the emoji plugin did, because that implementation is buggy and can lead to the application becoming unresponsive or even crashing in certain circumstances (when the regex matches strings that are not present in the map). The only stable way I've found to implement this, is by adding three separate transformers with exactly matching regex._x000D_
_x000D_
I've tested the changes on the Linux app and the Android app and I'm happy with the changes.

Title: Consider running node in foreground or periodically
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**_x000D_
Instead of running 24/7 from a resource consumption perspective it makes sense to run either when the user in using the node or periodically (e.g. to check F2F messages, or in the context of a chat application)._x000D_
_x000D_
**Describe the solution you'd like**_x000D_
I'd like the node to either run when I open the application to navigate / check messages etc. This will also mean that the "Start/Stop" functionally becomes implicit in the use. The application needs to redo it's UI to be content-first either by including FMS/Sone/Chat app etc.
Description: Running 24/7, it makes more sense to run it when the user is using the node or at regular intervals

Title: Improve text to speech UI
Body: Users should be able to ff or rewind.

Title: Save the state
Body:
Hi,

for the first look the app design lookes plain and simple (in a positive meaning). But with all simplicity there is one thing i miss, the possibility to save the state after the app has been ended (by whatever, maybe phone restart, maybe androids oom or maybe a task killer).

It lookes dumb to me to start everytime over from the beginning when the app forces me to. You dont need a save system with multiple slots (but there is on the other hand nothing wrong with this) but at least one should be there. Maybe you can just serialize all content of the activity when its get to background, save that to file and if it is restarter just load it again. Also dont forget a start over button... From my experiences that is the easiest, but still powerful, way to make thinks in permanent (over restart) in java based applications.

thanks in advance

Title: Extract media from les pas private storage ("export all"-) option #FeatureRequest
Body:
Hi @scubajeff,_x000D_
_x000D_
I know, that you probably thought about this a lot, since you mentioned your reasoning (privacy) in the readme file, but I wanted to give you 2 arguments for giving the user the option to store media in an external directory._x000D_
_x000D_
1. Preventing data loss. Uninstalling les pas would no longer remove all users media (see https://github.com/scubajeff/lespas/issues/56)._x000D_
2. Decoupling from nextcloud backend. Les pas could be used as a local gallery and album manager and the external media directory could be synced for example through syncthing (p2p) or foldersync (FTP, SSH, Samba, Webdav, a lot of cloud providers)._x000D_
_x000D_
I'm curious about your opinion.

Title: Better support for pre-downloading along GPX tracks
Body:
<!--_x000D_
Attaching screenshots / videos:_x000D_
Large images / videos can be very disturbing when reading, so please avoid attaching unnecessary screenshots and videos._x000D_
If you think they are needed, reduce the size or use thumbnails to keep the issue nicely readable._x000D_
-->_x000D_
_x000D_
**Use case**_x000D_
<!-- Provide a clear and concise description of *your use case* and what you thus think is missing, and why. -->_x000D_
_x000D_
SCEE allows (under  `Settings` / `Display` / `Display GPX track from file` /  `Download are covered by GPX track`) to pre-download data along some GPX track, which is very useful in situation when one plans to map areas where download might be too slow (like travelling in a bus/coach/trail), expensive (e.g. roaming), impossible (no mobile data in rural areas) or too battery draining etc. _x000D_
_x000D_
However, it currently only works on small areas, where its usability is limited (i.e. one could've just manually clicked on `Download data here` at that location), likely because of simplistic approach by trying to [download one bbox containing whole area](https://github.com/streetcomplete/StreetComplete/issues/473#issuecomment-1777929825) covered by GPX, which will then in majority of use cases fail with `Download area too big, please download manually`_x000D_
_x000D_
So one has to do it manually, which is extremely time consuming and tedious._x000D_
_x000D_
**Proposed Solution**_x000D_
It would be great if SCEE employed more advanced download strategy, which would allow to download quests for inter-city along track of inter-city coach or train and other bigger areas. That could be accomplished by splitting the download area in several smaller chunks, like e.g. [JOSM](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/JOSM) does._x000D_
_x000D_
Also, does that functionality downloads only quest or also missing background vector tiles? (one would want both, of course)_x000D_
_x000D_
example JOSM functionality (SCEE might not need all those tunables, but `download everything within XXX meters` might be useful):_x000D_
![josm](https://github.com/Helium314/SCEE/assets/156656/9eb778ee-a8d5-4ae8-a2f2-5f6c1bd771fb)_x000D_
_x000D_
_x000D_
<!-- _x000D_
There may be several different solutions for your idea or use case, add your deliberations about it here to help sort out the best one(s)._x000D_
Add screenshots or mockups to visualize your idea, if applicable._x000D_
-->_x000D_
_x000D_

Title: Update command args for live goes_hrit in official docs
Body:
Hello, I noticed that on the official documentation page for the command-line usage it has an example for goes_hrit that is missing an 'e6' after the frequency value. Currently if you just copy and paste the example you get 0 dB SNR output. With the added e6 it should read:_x000D_
_x000D_
`satdump live goes_hrit output_test_wtf --source rtlsdr --samplerate 2.4e6 --frequency 1694.1e6 --gain 40 --http_server 0.0.0.0:8080`_x000D_
_x000D_
The page its on: https://www.satdump.org/posts/basic-usage/#live-processing_x000D_
_x000D_
I wasn't sure where to report this, its so minor. I just had some trouble figuring it out when I blindly copied the command. Was hoping to save anyone else from the same trouble.

Title: Elektro L4 Misaligned Images with low SNR
Body:
I'm getting about 3-4 dB SNR on Elektro-L4, and images are coming in, but getting a perfect image is quite rare. Often the images are corrupted with misaligned sections._x000D_
_x000D_
This is using a 65cm discovery dish with linear feed. I realize the dish size might too small for Elektro, and also the feed is linear instead of circular, hence the SNR is probably too low resulting in some errors in the decoding. From what I've seen from others more than 6 dB is probably required for perfect images._x000D_
_x000D_
But is there any possible enhancements to the way SatDump stitches the segments together? For example in this image almost everything is there apart from some missing data in the lower right, and this appears to have caused a full misaligned segment. If the misaligned section was at least aligned the image would be usable._x000D_
_x000D_
![L4_8_20240112T093000Z](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/17711170/e7f31008-4d13-40e2-8ace-fd0a4ce198e4)_x000D_
![L4_4_20240112T123000Z](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/17711170/d896153d-5da2-44da-a0b3-31eb4a343f47)_x000D_
_x000D_
On the color images there is also often corruption with what looks like the color components not aligning._x000D_
_x000D_
![L4_221_20240112T063000Z](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/17711170/902bc503-c138-4f3a-a3b3-abfde892ea89)_x000D_
_x000D_
Also while I'm here, has anyone else noticed that while Elektro is circularly polarized, it also has a slight linear polarization to it? Optimizing the linear feed angle results in a difference of about 2 dB.

Title: Save record only when satellite is above selected minimum elevation
Body:
In Schedule and config menu there is presently an option about satellite minimum elevation._x000D_
This selects satellite passes with at least requested minimum elevation. Good._x000D_
However saved files on disk record the whole pass from AOS to LOS, not only when elevation is above minimum._x000D_
When using omnidirectional antenna such as a patch the signal SNR is not optimum due to low gain and satellite slant range. _x000D_
In order to reduce size of recorded files without interesting signal could it be possible to only save part of the pass over selected minimal elevation ?_x000D_
Here is a complete record displayed with Audacity, showing the part with stronger signal._x000D_
![Capture d’écran du 2024-01-13 11-44-45](https://github.com/SatDump/SatDump/assets/14106669/edbfce35-f1e1-48cb-87cc-94e0957b8b28)_x000D_

Title: Issues with FY-4A LRIT and HRIT live decode
Body:
I rotated my 1.8m dish from GK-2A HRIT over to FY-4A last weekend as it'd been a while since I had checked it. I attempted to get both FY-4A LRIT as well as HRIT. SNR looked good for both services, but no data was decoded, encrypted or in the clear. Historically when I looked at it with my 1.2m offset I was able to get sync and data from both services. LRIT Demodulator looks very strange, and HRIT SNR values are over 2dB higher than my prior attempts that were successful. I attempted decimation of both 2 and 3, and yet I still couldn't get data out. I have a couple cadus from HRIT btw, sadly they weren't end to end as I started the record a little bit after the transmission began. Let me know if you'd like to see them. _x000D_
_x000D_
Satdump in use was from the past 2 weeks (I update pretty regularly). Tested on both Debian 12 and Ubuntu 22.04LTS._x000D_

Title: Instant wind visualization/calculation
Body:
XCSoar version 7.36_x000D_
--------------_x000D_
_x000D_
I'd like to have an instant wind estimate, respectively an appropriate visualisation in case it is present. Instant wind I define here in contrast to a synoptic wind, that does not change much during a xc flight. An instant wind sensor senses the wind change in a below one minute time frame. The exact latency of an instant wind sensor is related to the signal to noise ratio of available pressure, GNSS and IMU inputs. Know examples of such sensors are hawk, anemoi and Larus. The latter certainly with the most expensive gnss and imu hardware and thus with the smallest latency around 5sec ([stefly](https://www.stefly.aero/product/larus_glider_sensor/) u. [Larus](https://github.com/larus-breeze) )._x000D_
_x000D_
What are you trying to do, what is the use case for the suggestion?_x000D_
-------------------------------------------------------------------_x000D_
Visualize the instant wind estimate on the current XCS display for various tactical flight decisions._x000D_
_x000D_
Suggestion would be:_x000D_
1. Same arrow as the current on circing around the glider symbol, but with a different color._x000D_
2. Arrow with a different radial position around the glider symbol._x000D_
3. Different graphical item/arrow_x000D_
_x000D_
Steps envisioned to get there:_x000D_
a) XCS should be able to read NMEA sentences that deliver instant wind and handle the info. I am currently enabling [XCV ](https://xcvario.de/)to convert the anemoi protocol to a standard NMEA 0182 sentences._x000D_
This first step will enable the more sophisticated second step._x000D_
_x000D_
b) Provide a software solution for the instant wind indication when an GNSS, IAS and AHRS source is connected to XCS. The calculation as said will depend on the quality of those sources and a first implementation will need some configuration parameter to handle inputs properly. The instant wind quality is expected to be inferior compared to a boxed solution with high quality seonsors, but still possible. The sources of the Larus Breeze project are available for integration as stated from the author and are GPL3._x000D_
_x000D_
Hope the merged feature request is now a bit more elaborate.

Title: [FEATURE] Manual location refresh?
Body:
Hello,

I've noticed that while locus is in background (swiped away from recent apps) it only updates once an hour which may be too long for many to wait for a location update (I thought it was supposed to be 15 minutes, if im not mistaken?)

Can you implement an option, that the user who creates the task must allow called "Manual location refresh" or something like it, where those who are viewing location can update their location, as that person may not be in same location anymore depending on when it was last updated.

Thats how Google maps works with location sharing feature. When user clicks to see friends location, google pings their device to get its location and sends it back. I believe its done this way to avoid further battery drain by constantly sending the location when its not needed.

Title: BLE Challenges,
Body:
BLE Challenges,_x000D_
_x000D_
When searching for new sensor the system find them during scan_x000D_
but fail or takes many tries to recognize them as having supported service._x000D_
_x000D_
Could not find the reason…._x000D_
_x000D_
We may have too many sensor around?_x000D_
==> Would it make sense to scan only for supported services? to improve probability of support?_x000D_
_x000D_
in BleScannerSDK21 add:_x000D_
_x000D_
    private final ScanSettings settings = new ScanSettings.Builder()_x000D_
            .setScanMode(ScanSettings.SCAN_MODE_LOW_LATENCY)_x000D_
            .build();_x000D_
_x000D_
  private final ScanFilter HrFilter = new ScanFilter.Builder()_x000D_
            .setServiceUuid(ParcelUuid.fromString("0000180d-0000-1000-8000-00805f9b34fb"))_x000D_
            .build();_x000D_
_x000D_
   private final ScanFilter CscFilter = new ScanFilter.Builder()_x000D_
            .setServiceUuid(ParcelUuid.fromString("00001816-0000-1000-8000-00805f9b34fb"))_x000D_
            .build();_x000D_
_x000D_
_x000D_
    private final ArrayList<ScanFilter> Uuidfilters = new ArrayList<ScanFilter>(Arrays.asList(HrFilter, CscFilter));_x000D_
_x000D_
_x000D_
and replace startScan parameters:_x000D_
_x000D_
                scanner.startScan(Uuidfilters, settings, callback);        _x000D_
//                scanner.startScan(callback);_x000D_
_x000D_
_x000D_
Issues are open on this subject:_x000D_
https://github.com/bailuk/AAT/issues/70_x000D_
_x000D_
Workaround (last resort) not very elegent: _x000D_
- Edit the configuration database with name & mac address  ;-(_x000D_
- Or configure the address of sensors?_x000D_
  _x000D_
——————_x000D_

After we have Sensors recognized as supported and enabled._x000D_
Getting them connected is complex exercise, We have « Connecting… » for a while._x000D_
Some time, rescan or disable & enable works?_x000D_
_x000D_
In order to keep connection and being able to work in parallel the system use _x000D_
connectGatt( context, « autoConnect » …)_x000D_
_x000D_
found that BLE on Android is not as straight forward as we would expect_x000D_
It looks that one of key element to connect with « autoConnect » is to have the sensor in the cash beforehand._x000D_
But we have difficulty to get there….._x000D_

Title: Refactor Member Ignoring Method smell in MyLocationActivity class
Body:
Hi, I'm Emanuele Iannone, a master student at University of Salerno.
Since my bachelor's thesis I have been working on a **code smell refactoring plugin** called *aDoctor*, which is able to identify and fix energy-related problems in Android apps.
I launched it on your project, finding different instances of code smells. I chose one of them and let the plugin automatically fix it.
In this case I chose Member Ignoring Method, that is present when a non static method does not use at all instance variables and other non static methods. These kind of smell may have a non trivial impact on energy consumption, as shown in this paper: `https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0950584918301678`.
Besides, this kind of refactoring does not impact on the functionalities of your app, so it is totally safe. Let me know if you are interested in this refactoring proposal.

Title: [Feature Request] Passively save my location history whenever possible (e.g. the phone is awaken), then send all of it on request
Body:
Not sure if without google services you can figure this out on android. But I think it would be amazing if this app had an option to track and store your route locally - but passively (e.g. to not actively awaken phone every minute to connect to gps and save a point, but rather to wait for either 1). the next time the phone is manually awaken by user, or 2). take advantage of any other automatic awake timers or events that android receives from other apps and other services, to also have Finder check for location (e.g. you get a new instant message and the screen lights up? Cool. Finder will also record a location point)._x000D_
_x000D_
Basically the motivation is to 1). have a personal log of where I've been, but without (that much) extra battery drain. And 2). whenever someone requests my location via SMS, my phone can send them all my locations for the past e.g. 8 hours instead of just one point. _x000D_
_x000D_
Would also gladly pay for such a feature. It would be super useful, especially when you're taking care of an older person._x000D_
_x000D_
Cheers.

Title: Pause-resume issues / Move detach to onDestroy?
Body:
I've been coming across a few niggly pause/resume issues (listed below)._x000D_
_x000D_
What do you think about moving the _getTileProvider().detach()_ from onPause to onDestroy?  I'm worried about memory leaks if I do this?  I tried running it with LeakCanary but it didn't show any memory leak after I stopped the app.  On the other hand it also didn't show a memory leak when I took the detach out altogether so I'm confused there..._x000D_
_x000D_
The code would need more work around the onResume / onPause but first I just wanted to ask what you thought about the general principle of moving the detach to onDestroy?_x000D_
_x000D_
Here are some of the issues:_x000D_
_x000D_
- Location Editor: map is lost when resuming after pausing, e.g. if permission request box comes up or if you go to the Home screen_x000D_
_x000D_
- If in Itinerary or Blog when pausing, screen goes to journey planner (or most recent ?resumable? fragment) when resuming, rather than back to Itinerary or Blog._x000D_
_x000D_
- If Find a Place marker is showing when pausing, it would disappear when resuming except that I have put in a workaround to store it.  This means is stays there even if app destroyed – not so good.  I could refine the workaround further but I'd prefer to improve the pause/resume and get rid of this workaround._x000D_
_x000D_
- If POI bubble is displayed on journey planner, the bubble disappears when resuming.  It also disappears if you go to another screen (e.g. Itinerary) and back._x000D_
_x000D_
(I think waymarkers are stored so that they remain if app paused or destroyed.  I think that's good to keep them even when app destroyed.)_x000D_
_x000D_
- When an item is selected from the Navigation Drawer (top left menu), a new instance of the fragment is created, meaning again that current state is lost (e.g. going from journey planner to Itinerary and back will mean that POI bubble disappears)._x000D_
_x000D_
- If the permission request box comes up this also pauses the app.  I would like the app to continue straight to the requested action if permission is granted but this is made more difficult by the fact that the fragment is destroyed and re-created when the app resumes.

Title: Refactor Member Ignoring Method smell in MapFragment class
Body:
Hi, I'm Emanuele Iannone, a master student at University of Salerno.
Since my bachelor's thesis I have been working on a **code smell refactoring plugin** called *aDoctor*, which is able to identify and fix energy-related problems in Android apps.
I launched it on your project, finding different instances of code smells. I chose one of them and let the plugin automatically fix it.
In this case I chose Member Ignoring Method, that is present when a non static method does not use at all instance variables and other non static methods. These kind of smell may have a non trivial impact on energy consumption, as shown in this paper: `https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0950584918301678`.
Besides, this kind of refactoring does not impact on the functionalities of your app, so it is totally safe. Let me know if you are interested in this refactoring proposal.

Title: Bump tzinfo from 1.2.5 to 2.0.5 in /data/suica
Body:
Bumps [tzinfo](https://github.com/tzinfo/tzinfo) from 1.2.5 to 2.0.5.
Release notes

Sourced from tzinfo's releases.

v2.0.5

  • Changed DateTime results to always use the proleptic Gregorian calendar. This affects DateTime results prior to 1582-10-15 and any arithmetic performed on the results that would produce a secondary result prior to 1582-10-15.
  • Added support for eager loading all the time zone and country data by calling either TZInfo::DataSource#eager_load! or TZInfo.eager_load!. Compatible with Ruby On Rails' eager_load_namespaces. #129.
  • Ignore the SECURITY file from Arch Linux's tzdata package. #134.

TZInfo v2.0.5 on RubyGems.org

v2.0.4

  • Fixed an incorrect InvalidTimezoneIdentifier exception raised when loading a zoneinfo file that includes rules specifying an additional transition to the final defined offset (for example, Africa/Casablanca in version 2018e of the Time Zone Database). #123.

TZInfo v2.0.4 on RubyGems.org

v2.0.3

  • Added support for handling "slim" format zoneinfo files that are produced by default by zic version 2020b and later. The POSIX-style TZ string is now used calculate DST transition times after the final defined transition in the file. #120.
  • Fixed TimeWithOffset#getlocal returning a TimeWithOffset with the timezone_offset still assigned when called with an offset argument on JRuby 9.3.
  • Rubinius is no longer supported.

TZInfo v2.0.3 on RubyGems.org

v2.0.2

  • Fixed 'wrong number of arguments' errors when running on JRuby 9.0. #114.
  • Fixed warnings when running on Ruby 2.8. #113.

TZInfo v2.0.2 on RubyGems.org

v2.0.1

  • Fixed "SecurityError: Insecure operation - require" exceptions when loading data with recent Ruby releases in safe mode. #100.
  • Fixed warnings when running on Ruby 2.7. #109.
  • Added a TZInfo::Timezone#=~ method that performs a regex match on the time zone identifier. #99.
  • Added a TZInfo::Country#=~ method that performs a regex match on the country code.

TZInfo v2.0.1 on RubyGems.org

v2.0.0

Added

  • to_local and period_for instance methods have been added to TZInfo::Timezone. These are similar to utc_to_local and period_for_utc, but take the UTC offset of the given time into account.
  • abbreviation, dst?, base_utc_offset and observed_utc_offset instance methods have been added to TZInfo::Timezone, returning the abbreviation, whether daylight savings time is in effect and the UTC offset of the time zone at a specified time.
  • A TZInfo::Timestamp class has been added. It can be used with TZInfo::Timezone in place of a Time or DateTime.
  • local_time, local_datetime and local_timestamp instance methods have been added to TZInfo::Timezone. These methods construct local Time, DateTime and TZInfo::Timestamp instances with the correct UTC offset and abbreviation for the time zone.
  • Support for a (yet to be released) version 2 of tzinfo-data has been added, in addition to support for version 1. The new version will remove the (no longer needed) DateTime parameters from transition times, reduce memory consumption and improve the efficiency of loading timezone and country indexes.
  • A TZInfo::VERSION constant has been added, indicating the TZInfo version number.

Changed

  • The minimum supported Ruby versions are now Ruby MRI 1.9.3, JRuby 1.7 (in 1.9 or later mode) and Rubinius 3.
  • Local times are now returned using the correct UTC offset (instead of using UTC). #49 and #52.
  • Local times are returned as instances of TimeWithOffset, DateTimeWithOffset or TZInfo::TimestampWithOffset. These classes subclass Time, DateTime and TZInfo::Timestamp respectively. They override the default behaviour of the base classes to return information about the observed offset at the indicated time. For example, the zone abbreviation is returned when using the %Z directive with strftime.
  • The transitions_up_to, offsets_up_to and strftime instance methods of TZInfo::Timezone now take the UTC offsets of given times into account (instead of ignoring them as was previously the case).
  • The TZInfo::TimezonePeriod class has been split into two subclasses: TZInfo::OffsetTimezonePeriod and TZInfo::TransitionsTimezonePeriod. TZInfo::OffsetTimezonePeriod is returned for time zones that only have a single offset. TZInfo::TransitionsTimezonePeriod is returned for periods that start or end with a transition.

... (truncated)

Changelog

Sourced from tzinfo's changelog.

Version 2.0.5 - 19-Jul-2022

  • Changed DateTime results to always use the proleptic Gregorian calendar. This affects DateTime results prior to 1582-10-15 and any arithmetic performed on the results that would produce a secondary result prior to 1582-10-15.
  • Added support for eager loading all the time zone and country data by calling either TZInfo::DataSource#eager_load! or TZInfo.eager_load!. Compatible with Ruby On Rails' eager_load_namespaces. #129.
  • Ignore the SECURITY file from Arch Linux's tzdata package. #134.

Version 2.0.4 - 16-Dec-2020

  • Fixed an incorrect InvalidTimezoneIdentifier exception raised when loading a zoneinfo file that includes rules specifying an additional transition to the final defined offset (for example, Africa/Casablanca in version 2018e of the Time Zone Database). #123.

Version 2.0.3 - 8-Nov-2020

  • Added support for handling "slim" format zoneinfo files that are produced by default by zic version 2020b and later. The POSIX-style TZ string is now used calculate DST transition times after the final defined transition in the file. #120.
  • Fixed TimeWithOffset#getlocal returning a TimeWithOffset with the timezone_offset still assigned when called with an offset argument on JRuby 9.3.
  • Rubinius is no longer supported.

Version 2.0.2 - 2-Apr-2020

  • Fixed 'wrong number of arguments' errors when running on JRuby 9.0. #114.
  • Fixed warnings when running on Ruby 2.8. #113.

Version 2.0.1 - 24-Dec-2019

  • Fixed "SecurityError: Insecure operation - require" exceptions when loading data with recent Ruby releases in safe mode. #100.
  • Fixed warnings when running on Ruby 2.7. #109.
  • Added a TZInfo::Timezone#=~ method that performs a regex match on the time zone identifier. #99.
  • Added a TZInfo::Country#=~ method that performs a regex match on the country code.

Version 2.0.0 - 26-Dec-2018

... (truncated)

Commits
  • d9b289e Preparing v2.0.5.
  • 264c763 Add v0.3.61 and v1.2.10 from the 0.3 and 1.2 branches.
  • ca29f34 Fix relative path loading tests.
  • c4f177c Add a top level eager_load! method for Rails compatibility.
  • 94be919 Support preloading all data from a DataSource.
  • fe7ad26 Clarify that both files and directories are excluded.
  • d2883cf Tidy up of security file ignoring.
  • 6a4766e Merge pull request #133.
  • 5d53b59 Workaround for 'Permission denied - NUL' errors with JRuby on Windows.
  • 83450a1 ignore SECURITY file for Arch tzdata package
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

Title: patch to fix data loss during manage categories activity
Body:
Hello developers of privacy friendly notes_x000D_
_x000D_
I am using your app privacy friendly notes. I think the app is great but I have one minor patch that could improve the user experience._x000D_
_x000D_
Here is a picture to help illustrate what activity that are changed in my patch:_x000D_
_x000D_
https://ibb.co/yVybm14_x000D_
_x000D_
When the user tries to add a new category. If the screen focus goes to another app or activity or if the user accidentally closes or press back the app, the user will lose any data they had put into this page_x000D_
_x000D_
This feature will automatically store the data when the user leaves the activity without submitting and restore said data when they restart the activity(this can be seen here where I closed the activity and restarted it https://ibb.co/cCdw2CG). Therefore, the user does not have to fill in the data again thus improving the user experience._x000D_
_x000D_
I have tested out the feature to ensure it works._x000D_
_x000D_
If you have any questions or if you would like me to change anything, please do not hesitate to let me know!_x000D_
_x000D_
Thank you for your time,_x000D_
Tim

Title: Refactor Member Ignoring Method smell in Shell class
Body:
Hi, I'm Emanuele Iannone, a master student at University of Salerno.
Since my bachelor's thesis I have been working on a **code smell refactoring plugin** called *aDoctor*, which is able to identify and fix energy-related problems in Android apps.
I launched it on your project, finding different instances of code smells. I chose one of them and let the plugin automatically fix it.
In this case I chose Member Ignoring Method, that is present when a non static method does not use at all instance variables and other non static methods. These kind of smell may have a non trivial impact on energy consumption, as shown in this paper: `https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0950584918301678`.
Besides, this kind of refactoring does not impact on the functionalities of your app, so it is totally safe. Let me know if you are interested in this refactoring proposal.

Release notes

Sourced from kotlinx-coroutines-core's releases.

Title: FR: Multiple metronomes, more logical notation editor
Body:
1. It would be really nice to be able to have multiple metronomes going on simultaneously to practice multiple rhythmic patterns. This is mainly for polyrhythms and polymeters(such as 3 against 7 or 5 against 4)._x000D_
_x000D_
Maybe as sample way to add this feature would be to abstract the metronome so that multiple can be run._x000D_
_x000D_
Then add an overlay or easy way to add or remove multiple instances. _x000D_
_x000D_
Then it is just a matter of synchronizing the metronomes(so some work on dealing with how the sounds are played is in order. Probably the easiest way is to render the waveform directly to an audio buffer and the ship the buffer to the audio output. The main issue here is to make sure everything is nearly perfectly synced so there are no weird timing issues between the different instances._x000D_
_x000D_
2. Add some ability to add a latency to the sound including negative. The issue is that there is some delay from the animation effects and the sound itself and if one is watching the metronome it can be a little discombobulating._x000D_
 _x000D_
3. The notation editor is a nice feature but it is a little strange to use. It would be better if it was more like standard music notation editors in which a beat is literally is specified by the time signature. Then a beat can be divided in a natural way. Currently one has to "add notes" but a note is, tempo speaking, an arbitrary unit specified by the notation rather than specified by the meter/bar.  E.g.,  if I add 3 "units" those can be anything I say. When a quarter note is selected and turns it in to an eighth note it will change time signature(say from 4/4 to 7/8)._x000D_
_x000D_
Maybe adding an "optional logic" for more standard notation would be better than to replace the current system. This way it could be done gradually. I'm mainly interested in the first request so I can practice polyrhythms more accurately._x000D_
_x000D_
