09-08 15:59:23.659  1017   699 28594 W keystore2: keystore2::watchdog: and all 2 records below._x000D_
09-08 15:59:23.659  1017   699 28594 W keystore2: keystore2::watchdog: ThreadId(19) IKeystoreOperation::update Pending: 2.501168436s Overdue 2.001173229s_x000D_
09-08 15:59:23.659  1017   699 28594 W keystore2: keystore2::watchdog: ThreadId(19) Operation::update: calling update Pending: 2.501141822s Overdue 2.001142707s_x000D_
09-08 15:59:23.659  1017   699 28594 W keystore2: keystore2::watchdog: ### Keystore Watchdog report - END ###_x000D_
09-08 15:59:24.659  1017   699 28594 W keystore2: keystore2::watchdog: ### Keystore Watchdog report - BEGIN ###_x000D_
09-08 15:59:24.659  1017   699 28594 W keystore2: keystore2::watchdog: When extracting from a bug report, please include this header_x000D_
09-08 15:59:24.660  1017   699 28594 W keystore2: keystore2::watchdog: and all 2 records below._x000D_
09-08 15:59:24.660  1017   699 28594 W keystore2: keystore2::watchdog: ThreadId(19) IKeystoreOperation::update Pending: 3.502164999s Overdue 3.002170416s_x000D_
09-08 15:59:24.660  1017   699 28594 W keystore2: keystore2::watchdog: ThreadId(19) Operation::update: calling update Pending: 3.502149582s Overdue 3.002150623s_x000D_
09-08 15:59:24.660  1017   699 28594 W keystore2: keystore2::watchdog: ### Keystore Watchdog report - END ###_x000D_
```_x000D_
_x000D_
Looking at the backtrace of the main thread at the moment of the ANR, we can see that Aegis is communicating with the Keystore service to decrypt the biometric key slot. We see something similar when they're trying to set up biometrics through Aegis' settings menu._x000D_
_x000D_

  native: #06 pc 0000000000175bb8  /system/lib64/libandroid_runtime.so (android_os_BinderProxy_transact(_JNIEnv*, _jobject*, int, _jobject*, _jobject*, int)+156) (BuildId: 9fd640b0990deb09d17a2a91aa61b073)_x000D_

  at android.security.KeyStoreOperation$$ExternalSyntheticLambda1.execute(unavailable:4)_x000D_
  at android.security.KeyStoreOperation.handleExceptions(KeyStoreOperation.java:69)_x000D_
  at android.security.KeyStoreOperation.update(KeyStoreOperation.java:115)_x000D_
  at android.security.keystore2.KeyStoreCryptoOperationChunkedStreamer$MainDataStream.update(KeyStoreCryptoOperationChunkedStreamer.java:222)_x000D_
  at android.security.keystore2.KeyStoreCryptoOperationChunkedStreamer.update(KeyStoreCryptoOperationChunkedStreamer.java:156)_x000D_
  at android.security.keystore2.KeyStoreCryptoOperationChunkedStreamer.doFinal(KeyStoreCryptoOperationChunkedStreamer.java:179)_x000D_
  at android.security.keystore2.AndroidKeyStoreAuthenticatedAESCipherSpi$BufferAllOutputUntilDoFinalStreamer.doFinal(AndroidKeyStoreAuthenticatedAESCipherSpi.java:396)_x000D_
  at android.security.keystore2.AndroidKeyStoreCipherSpiBase.engineDoFinal(AndroidKeyStoreCipherSpiBase.java:603)_x000D_

  at kotlin.LazyKt__LazyKt.decrypt(SourceFile:18)_x000D_
  at com.beemdevelopment.aegis.vault.slots.RawSlot.getKey(SourceFile:7)_x000D_
  at androidx.biometric.BiometricViewModel$1.onAuthenticationSucceeded(SourceFile:28)_x000D_
  at androidx.core.app.ActivityRecreator$1.run(SourceFile:197)_x000D_
  at android.os.Handler.handleCallback(Handler.java:942)_x000D_
  at android.os.Handler.dispatchMessage(Handler.java:99)_x000D_

  at android.os.Looper.loop(Looper.java:475)_x000D_
  at android.app.ActivityThread.main(ActivityThread.java:7950)_x000D_

```_x000D_
_x000D_
It would not be a surprise that waiting for multiple seconds for a response from the Keystore service, on the main thread, is eventually going to result in Android finding that your app is not responsive. We should consider moving this off the main thread. I still don't understand why the user keeps getting ANR popups though, even after the app is responsive again.
Description: Waiting for a response from the Keystore service takes seconds on the main thread, eventually causing Android to find your application unresponsive

Title: Application unresponsive when cancelling opening a book.
Body:
# Steps to reproduce_x000D_
- Try to open a book. Preferably a large one, like the one attached._x000D_
- Wait a few seconds and cancel opening it, by pressing the x/cross icon inside of the "Please wait" message._x000D_
- A blank screen is shown. The application appears unresponsive. Sometimes, a system message might even appear recommending to kill the app. If you wait some time, librera will correctly close the book and come back to the library view._x000D_
_x000D_
If there is some reason that eludes me why closing books in such manner has to be slow, at the very least librera should indicate that it has not crashed. For example a new message "Please wait, closing the book" should appear._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_

Title: Show source metadata ASAP when creating annotation from share
Body: Currently, when creating a new annotation from share, the behavior is to show the splash screen until the source is initialized. This results in what looks like an unresponsive application if the source takes longer than a couple seconds to load. Instead, a better layout would be to immediately show the toolbar and webview with the in-toolbar indeterminate loader displayed during initialization.

Title: App freezes on load after not using for extended period of time
Body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Do not open app for several hours (e.g. 12)_x000D_
2. Launch app_x000D_
3. App remains unresponsive for an extended period (often over a minute)

### Expected behavior

The app should remain responsive and actually work.

### Actual behavior

The app is frozen. I cannot scroll, press buttons, etc. It's unusable at that point and I'm forced to use the "Force stop app" button when it comes up, or kill the app manually and try launching it again.

### LibreTube version

0.19.0

### Android version

Android 10

### Other details

I typically have my phone in Medium Power Saving Mode._x000D_
_x000D_
Also, the number of subscriptions may have something to do with this. You get a ton of notifications in an instant after killing and relaunching the app, which is why I think channel subscription count might be related.

### Acknowledgements

- [X] I have searched the existing issues and this is a new ticket, **NOT** a duplicate or related to another open issue.
- [X] I have written a short but informative title.
- [X] I will fill out all of the requested information in this form.

Title: ANR when typing after selecting link
Body:
### Steps to reproduce_x000D_
1. Install [this notetaking app](https://f-droid.org/en/packages/com.maltaisn.notes.sync/) from F-droid. [This](https://f-droid.org/repo/com.maltaisn.notes.sync_10503.apk) is the apk if you prefer a direct link._x000D_
2. Press the `+` button at the bottom right, to make a note._x000D_
3. Type `example.org   hello` as the note content (below the title)._x000D_
4. Press ASK's left arrow. This selects the link._x000D_
   4.1. The fact that the link is selected is reported [here](https://github.com/maltaisn/another-notes-app/issues/127), but I think that ASK should not give an ANR in any case._x000D_
5. While the link is selected,_x000D_
5.1. if you type `(` then ASK freezes and there is the ANR `Reason: Input dispatching timed out (Waiting to send non-key event because the touched window has not finished processing certain input events that were delivered to it over 500.0ms ago.  Wait queue length: 14.  Wait queue head age: 79937.5ms.)`_x000D_
5.2. if you have typed a letter instead of `(`, it has replaced the link text, as expected._x000D_
7. To restart ASK, I open another app, focus a text box, wait a few seconds._x000D_
_x000D_
This happens with Italian and German language packs on ASK 1.11.7137 + Samsung Android 11, Samsung Android 7.1._x000D_
_x000D_
(edited for clarity)

Title: Add a metronome
Body:
Having a metronome built into the session would be nice. Using the built in looping functionality of MediaPlayer and SoundPool to play the click has too much latency, but the [NDK](https://developer.android.com/ndk/guides/audio) will probably work._x000D_
_x000D_
Edit: the app works great in split screen mode with any other metronome app open, so I don't think this feature will be required.

Title: App is extremly slowing down with big cache (media exchange)
Body:
We have the problem, that if we exchange many pictures or videos in a room, the app cache grows and grows. After a size of 1-2 GB the app gets really slow and sending message is delayed for 2-4 seconds._x000D_
Clearing the cache makes the app fast again, but if the cache grows again, it gets more and more unresponsive._x000D_
_x000D_
It is hard to reproduce after cleaning the cache._x000D_
_x000D_
Maybe it is related to:_x000D_
https://github.com/element-hq/element-android/issues/8446_x000D_
_x000D_

Title: Acode loses connection and doesn't notify
Body:
A few times a day (for the past couple of weeks), and today several times in an hour, Acode appeared to lose its connection to my server and did not attempt to reconnect, nor did it produce any error when the file saves were unsuccessful. _x000D_
_x000D_
If the file browser is open, the directory name sometimes flashes but not always. (BTW: Even if that indicator consistently worked, I normally hide the tree while editing.)  Perhaps it doesn't always flash because the disconnect is not disconnected, or perhaps the connection is fine and the saving function is unresponsive for some reason. Either way, the yellow "unsaved" dot is removed, so there's no visual indication of the problem. I only know after trying to test my code for ten minutes and start wondering why my changes aren't appearing._x000D_
_x000D_
The only resolution is to restart and lose my changes. Or if I am certain the code is working, I need to copy the content of the files and paste them back to the files when a new connection is established._x000D_
_x000D_

ChromeOS v. 118.0.5993.86

Title: [mac] Godot Editor Stops Responding When Trying to Save & Close After Moving Project Location
Body:
### Tested versions

v4.2.1.stable.official [b09f793f5]

### System information

Mac 14.2.1 (23C71) - Apple M1 Max 

### Issue description

When editing a Godot project and moving the project folder outside the editor (ex: rename the root project folder), attempting to save-and-quit results in an unresponsive app that I need to force-quit to close.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a project_x000D_
2. Confirm project in file location_x000D_
3. Edit project_x000D_
  a. Change something in project so a save will need to trigger when trying to close_x000D_
4. In terminal, change the project folder to new name (ex: `test` -> `test_changed`)_x000D_
5. From the Godot editor, hit Cmd + Q to initiate a close_x000D_
6. Click `Save & Close`_x000D_
7. Observe beachball of death as the application stops responding_x000D_
_x000D_
_x000D_
Side note: I have been able to reproduce this a few times, but once it had a pop-up saying that the project had moved, but upon clicking `Ok` button, the same unresponsiveness happened

Works on any newly created project from (I tested on 4.2.1 and it had the same behavior regardless of render type)_x000D_

Title: Version 48
Body:
Version 48 is finally in the works after over an entire year of complete silence._x000D_
Here's what will come in the next update:_x000D_
_x000D_
- ~~An updated DeSmuME core. We will be upgrading it from 0.9.8 to 0.9.11.~~ Done_x000D_
- Lightning JIT will be upgraded from 1.2 to 2.1.2 to get the official ARM backend. The "ArmLJit" thing will have to be rewritten for that, provided I can actually figure out how to do that._x000D_
- Android devices running a x86 or x64 CPU may use AsmJIT instead, while ARMv7-A and ARMv8-A (AArch64) will use the updated Lightning JIT._x000D_
- Dropbox Sync (useful!), as seen on nds4ios and iNDS for iOS devices._x000D_
- ~~An improved UI.~~ Just switched to the Material design to look more "modern" and less "I'm old and from Ice Cream Sandwich."_x000D_
- An OpenGL ES 3.0 Renderer (as proposed on @Tsunamical's wish list, I believe)._x000D_
- ~~The threaded interpreter may be removed, due to its sluggish performance.~~ 47.1 is much faster on the normal DeSmuME interpreter, interestingly enough. JIT and the threaded interpreter don't._x000D_
- ~~True 64-bit ARM support will be added to increase performance for 64-bit devices.~~ Done_x000D_
- ~~We may switch to using Clang to compile the code, as GCC has been deprecated by Google.~~ Switched to Clang. GCC is going to be removed in the next major NDK release._x000D_
_x000D_
Not all devices can support OpenGL ES 3.0. It is recommended that you use CPU-Z or a similar app to find out if your device can handle it._x000D_
_x000D_
That's all for what will come for the next version of nds4droid. This is subject to change, but let's hope nothing on this list gets removed.

Title: Fast search for larger files
Body:
### Issue_x000D_
_x000D_
Searching for text using a text file that holds few kilobytes of data results in the app become unresponsive, extremely sluggish and very slow._x000D_
_x000D_
### Cause_x000D_
_x000D_
The issue is cause by the `EditText.highlightText()`.  Whenever a user tries to search for a string, this function tries to finds all instances of the string and replace them with `SpannableString`.  More specifically, `EditText.setText()` function is extremely slow when setting `SpannableText`:  this function is called for every search instance found._x000D_
_x000D_
### Solution_x000D_
_x000D_
Instead of highlighting all search instances, the code now just highlights the first result and once the next button is clicked it will highlight the next result and so on._x000D_
_x000D_
Additionally `EditText.setText()` function appears to be faster then `BackgroundColorSpan` is used instead of `SpannableText`._x000D_
Description: The EditText.setText() function is very slow in setting SpannableText: this function is called once for each search instance found. The code now highlights only the first search result instead of all search instances, and once the Next button is clicked, it highlights the next result, and so on.

Title: Freezes on reboot on Magisk Delta

Title: Notch compatibility mode forces wrong aspect ratio; makes device unresponsive
Body:
Using the option creates a tiny landscape screen in a portrait phone, and the touchscreen is entirely unresponsive. In that state, I can only escape by long-pressing the power button. _x000D_
_x000D_
However, the whole notch problem can be solved by going to the settings and choosing the option to hide the notch. Maybe that could be a better solution? The app has permission to modify system settings anyway.

Title: Slow response
Body: I tried the application it's really fantastic bit the response is really slow

Title: Getting history from graph or list in asynctask to avoid ANR
Body:
Problem with the history for sensor used in graph or list._x000D_
As it is done in main thread and not asynctask, if network or admin didn't reponse fast it ends up with an ANR error._x000D_
_x000D_
` at rinor.Rest_com.connect_jsonobject(Rest_com.java:73)`_x000D_
_x000D_
> response = httpclient.execute(httpget);_x000D_
Description: There is a problem with sensors using the history of charts or lists. Since it is executed in the main thread instead of an asynchronous task, ANR errors can occur if the network or administrator does not respond quickly.

Title: Version 1.2.0 very unresponsive, compared to 1.1.1
Body:
I was using version 1.1.1, it runs great with no problems or delays. Now I installed version 1.2.0 and when I press the flashlight toggle button, it has around 1 second long delay before the flashlight actually lights up. Same delay happens when I turn it off.

Also could you make an option so that the screen doesn't become white when I turn on the LED?

I am using Samsung Galaxy Ace GT-S5830i with Android Gingerbread 2.3.6

Title: App freezes when opening certain channels
Body:
#### Summary_x000D_
_x000D_
We have a few dozen channels in our Mattermost instance and when opening some of them on the mobile app, it shows the latest messages, but before it shows the blue header bar with the number of unread messages, the app freezes. After a few seconds, it shows a popup where I can decide to either wait, close the app or report a problem._x000D_
_x000D_
For months, I've always used "waiting" and I have to do that for up to 10 minutes sometimes, before the app comes back and works OK again. Since nothing changed for a long time, I've decided to report this bug with the following error log:_x000D_
_x000D_

Title: Long loading times on startup and channel load
Body:
#### Summary_x000D_
Loading takes longer than in previous versions, especially while loading channel contents._x000D_
_x000D_
### Environment Information_x000D_
  - Device Name: HTC One m9, Doogee S88+_x000D_
  - OS Version: LineageOS Android 9, Android 10_x000D_
  - Mattermost App Version: 2.9.1_x000D_
  - Mattermost Server Version: 9.1.0_x000D_
_x000D_
#### Steps to reproduce_x000D_
Load a server with multiple channels and direct messages, observe loading times._x000D_
_x000D_
Stop app, open via push notification of unread message, observe almost a minute of loading on slower devices._x000D_
_x000D_
#### Expected behavior_x000D_
Previous versions of the app had much faster loading, especially of channel content._x000D_
_x000D_
#### Observed behavior (that appears unintentional)_x000D_
Loading of the app itself is reasonably fast with the recent server versions, but loading channels takes a very long time, especially if the content is complex and already read. Channels with a lot of unread content tend to load faster for some reason. Even returning from a direct message to the channel list is often very slow._x000D_
_x000D_
It is worst when the app has been dormant or stopped entirely. The initial loading tends to be the biggest hurdle for it. The server and phone are located within the same network over 2.4ghz wifi and the server isn't experiencing any overload or other bottlenecks. The phone is a slightly older model, but newer devices show a similar behavior albeit less extreme(the relation of app and channel load times are the same just overall faster)_x000D_
_x000D_
This started a few versions ago. I noticed the overall layout and design changes in the app which went along with really slow loading. Over time this got a bit better, but it is definitely still sluggish even on powerful devices. I did check server logs for any major errors, but can't see any errors come in when the app starts loading._x000D_
_x000D_
Some history of this is in ticket #7001 Opening separate ticket to de-clutter as per @larkox request.

Title: Fix the sidebar calendar being unresponsive to size changes
Body:
The days of the sidebar calendar now shrinks & grows to meet the size of the calendar itself. In addition, a carousel component was added which features ARIA compliance._x000D_
_x000D_
Closes #6328.

Title: Add dummy "Loading" notification before actual player notification is ready
Body:
### Checklist

- [X] I made sure that there are *no existing issues* - [open](https://github.com/TeamNewPipe/NewPipe/issues) or [closed](https://github.com/TeamNewPipe/NewPipe/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) - which I could contribute my information to.
- [X] I have read the [FAQ](https://newpipe.net/FAQ/) and my problem isn't listed.
- [X] I'm aware that this is a request for NewPipe itself and that requests for adding a new service need to be made at [NewPipeExtractor](https://github.com/TeamNewPipe/NewPipeExtractor/issues).
- [X] I have taken the time to fill in all the required details. I understand that the feature request will be dismissed otherwise.
- [X] This issue contains only one feature request.
- [X] I have read and understood the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/TeamNewPipe/NewPipe/blob/dev/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md).

### Feature description

Before the actual media info is ready to load into the player media notification, the app should show a "Loading media" dummy notification if possible.

### Why do you want this feature?

As of 0.26.0, the app creates an "Unknown - Unknown" notification that can stay for up to 10+ seconds, until it gets populated with the actual media info. This is especially noticeable when background playback is used without loading the video page first._x000D_
_x000D_
This is a very long duration to stay unresponsive, which can cause confusion for users, making them think something is wrong.

### Additional information

Opened as per https://github.com/TeamNewPipe/NewPipe/pull/10712#issuecomment-1873233213.

Title: PulseAudio doesn't create /tmp/snapfifo pipe
Body:
**Describe the bug**_x000D_
I'm trying to run Snapcast on Raspberry Pi that acts as a Bluetooth receiver. I want to pair my phone with the Pi, stream music via Bluetooth and redirect the stream to Snapcast's `/tmp/snapfifo` pipe. The problem is that after following the [steps for PulseAudio](https://github.com/badaix/snapcast/blob/develop/doc/player_setup.md#pulseaudio) the stream is never redirected to Snapcast, because PulseAudio doesn't even create the named pipe._x000D_
_x000D_
**Steps to Reproduce**_x000D_
1. First I verified that BT streaming works without Snapcast. I'm using https://github.com/nicokaiser/rpi-audio-receiver to convert RPi to a Bluetooth receiver and it works without any issues when streaming music to the built-in audio card._x000D_
2. I added the lines from [the guideline](https://github.com/badaix/snapcast/blob/develop/doc/player_setup.md#pulseaudio) to my `/etc/pulse/system.pa`_x000D_
```_x000D_
..._x000D_
load-module module-pipe-sink file=/tmp/snapfifo sink_name=Snapcast format=s16le rate=48000_x000D_
update-sink-proplist Snapcast device.description=Snapcast_x000D_
```_x000D_
3. I restarted the pulseaudio systemd service. I also tried rebooting._x000D_
4. I confirmed with `pactl list sinks` that the PulseAudio sink is created and is set to RUNNING when I start streaming music._x000D_
```_x000D_
Sink #1_x000D_
	State: RUNNING_x000D_
	Name: Snapcast_x000D_
	Description: Snapcast_x000D_
	Driver: module-pipe-sink.c_x000D_
	Sample Specification: s16le 2ch 48000Hz_x000D_
	Channel Map: front-left,front-right_x000D_
	Owner Module: 15_x000D_
	Mute: no_x000D_
	Volume: front-left: 65536 / 100% / 0.00 dB,   front-right: 65536 / 100% / 0.00 dB_x000D_
	       balance 0.00_x000D_
	Base Volume: 65536 / 100% / 0.00 dB_x000D_
	Monitor Source: Snapcast.monitor_x000D_
	Latency: 226208 usec, configured 21333 usec_x000D_
	Flags: DECIBEL_VOLUME LATENCY _x000D_
	Properties:_x000D_
		device.string = "/tmp/snapfifo"_x000D_
		device.description = "Snapcast"_x000D_
		device.icon_name = "audio-card"_x000D_
	Formats:_x000D_
		pcm_x000D_
```_x000D_
5. I checked if snapcast received the stream to the pipe, but there's nothing. The pipe isn't even created, or if I create it manually it's not being written to by PulseAudio._x000D_
_x000D_
Are the guidelines outdated? Or is my PulseAudio setup still somewhat misconfigured?_x000D_
_x000D_
**Environment details**_x000D_
- OS: Raspberry Pi OS Lite 64-bit_x000D_
- Snapcast version: 0.27_x000D_
- Installed from a armhf.deb package._x000D_

Title: ALSA playback fails when outputting to dshares since v0.23.0
Body:
**Describe the bug**_x000D_
Snapclient cannot playback via ALSA PCM when the output device (-s) is a dshare. Snapclient will report repeated messages suggesting the PCM device is in an invalid state. Bug can be traced to the change to alsa_player.cpp at commit 273ad9622847ee8fa73d5de9de6bea99b34d352f. Bug persists through the current commit. Commenting out the offending code block from the current commit restores playback functionality. _x000D_
_x000D_
**Steps to Reproduce**_x000D_
1. Set up a dshare in /etc/asound.conf to split a USB sound card into multiple outputs_x000D_
2. Run snapclient and target a dshare output device._x000D_
3. Observe no playback and error messages in the logfile._x000D_
_x000D_
**Environment details**_x000D_

Title: App crash causes timers to disappears in app but notification persistent as unresponsive
Body:
### Steps to reproduce_x000D_
_x000D_
1. Create Timer_x000D_
2. do something that cause the app to crash_x000D_
3. reopen app_x000D_
_x000D_
### Expected behavior_x000D_
_x000D_
Timer to still be there or timer removed from notification_x000D_
_x000D_
Preferablely the formal, but as per #87, probably the later at the very least._x000D_
_x000D_
### Actual behavior_x000D_
_x000D_
If app crash, see old timer still in notification and still counting down but buttons don't work and it's not visible in app._x000D_
_x000D_
Sorry video too big, had to speed up by 2x to reduce duration/size._x000D_
_x000D_
https://github.com/you-apps/ClockYou/assets/4752645/6b927472-7703-4678-832f-87300952ffda_x000D_
_x000D_
### Clock You version_x000D_
_x000D_
6.4_x000D_
_x000D_
### Android version_x000D_
_x000D_
Android 13_x000D_
_x000D_
### Other details_x000D_
_x000D_
I have not found a way to crash the app reliably._x000D_
_x000D_
The main way it happen regularly (not 100%) for me is by exiting the app using the home screen, turn phone to sleep using the power button, wait for some undetermined amount of time, turn phone on, open app, see app hang, app crash after awhile._x000D_
edit: or maybe it's not always due to crashing but instead is due to being force closed by system after being inactive for sometime._x000D_
_x000D_
note: timer also disappear on force close, by system or user._x000D_
related #87 _x000D_
maybe related?  'Persistent timers' #146 seem like it's about preset timer, in which case it's misleadingly named._x000D_
_x000D_
_x000D_

Title: Ability to watch VOD broken since 2.10.3, applies to 2.10.4
Body:
**Describe the bug**_x000D_
Trying to view vod results in phone becoming unresponsive on both my s22+ (android 13, one ui 5.0) and s10+ (android 13, one ui 5.0) complete with "Twire isn't responding: close app or wait ". Waiting does nothing as the app never becomes responsive. I recently updated from 2.10.2 to 2.10.4 only to find that I could no longer watch vods. I've tried several things to fix the problem including: clearing cache, clearing data, uninstalling and reinstalling app, redownloading app, turning off vod chat(vod chat doesn't work btw), installing 2.10.3 (which also has this exact same problem), restarting phone, power off resetting phone. Both phones are completely up to date. Problem doesn't exist with live streams. Seems there is something special about vods._x000D_
_x000D_
**To Reproduce**_x000D_
Steps to reproduce the behavior:_x000D_
1. From Followed Streamers select Aurateur (the best streamer)._x000D_
2. Click on Broadcasts._x000D_
3. Click first video to appear._x000D_
4. Watch as phone instantly becomes unresponsive._x000D_
5. Give around 30 seconds for Twire isn't responding error message to show._x000D_
6. Cry._x000D_
_x000D_
**Expected behavior**_x000D_
Being able to watch vods would be nice. Not causing my phone to pop twire isn't responding would also be nice._x000D_
_x000D_
**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**_x000D_
 - Device: Samsung Galaxy s22+ and Samsung Galaxy s10+ (same problem on both)_x000D_
 - Android Version: Android 13, One UI 5.0_x000D_
 - Twire Version: 2.10.3 & 2.10.4_x000D_
_x000D_
**Additional context / screenshots**_x000D_
Both phones have this problem so there is consistency here. I will be rolling back to 2.10.2 until this is fixed as this is completely unacceptable to me._x000D_

Title: Major battery drain in 5.8.20
Body: After upgrading from 5.8.0 my phone started experiencing massive battery drain, and android blames it on this app. If I kill the app, the drain stops. Android 12, LineageOS 19.1, microg, moto g7.
